reasoning: All hypotheses of the informal statement are rendered correctly: n ≥ 3, x, y in R^n (as EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)), ‖x - y‖ = d, d > 0, r > 0, 2r > d. The conclusion "infinitely many z with |z-x|=|z-y|=r" is faithfully encoded as Set.Infinite of the set of such z. The only oddity is an unused parameter `z` in the binder list, but since the `z` in the set-builder is locally bound and EuclideanSpace is always inhabited, this extra universally-quantified unused variable is logically equivalent to the statement without it — it neither strengthens nor weakens the claim.
